title: 3 Federal Firefighters Just Died in a Burnover
script: The shelter didn't work? Three federal wildland firefighters are dead after a burnover on the Colorado-Utah border Saturday, where two merging wildfires became the Snyder Fire and swallowed their position faster than any shelter could stop. They'd deployed emergency fire shelters — the last-resort metallic blankets designed to reflect heat when there's nowhere left to run. Two others survived, burned. Winds were gusting to 44 miles per hour. Humidity was 2%. Fuel moisture readings were between 2 and 8 percent — basically kindling. The Snyder Fire had been burning at 0 percent containment when the skies turned. Governors of both Utah and Colorado declared emergencies. The names of the three firefighters are still being withheld pending family notifications. So who sends people into those conditions?
